channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Middle East latest: Palestinian leaders in the West Bank say they should run Gaza
item.description: The Palestinian prime minister says his authorities in the occupied West Bank are the only ones who should run the Gaza Strip in the future, as Israel and Hamas appear to be at the closest point yet to sealing a deal to end 15 months of war. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Africa tried to pressure informal gold miners by cutting off food. Now it's pulling out bodies
item.description: STILFONTEIN, South Africa (AP) — Rescuers and volunteers have pulled at least 78 dead miners and more than 160 survivors from an abandoned South African gold mine, where they have languished for over two months during a standoff with authorities who demanded they surrender to police because they wer
item.dc:creator: MOGOMOTSI MAGOME and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel supplied Iran with centrifuge platforms containing explosives, top official acknowledges
item.description: Israel supplied Iran with centrifuge platforms containing explosives for its nuclear enrichment program in an act of sabotage.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Florida Democrats rip Biden for lifting Cuba terror sponsor designation
item.description: The Florida Democratic Party slammed President Biden for his decision to lift Cuba’s State Sponsors of Terrorism (SST) designation in exchange for freeing jailed political prisoners who protested the regime in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: India's navy launches submarine, warships to guard against China's presence in Indian Ocean
item.description: MUMBAI, India (AP) — India's navy on Wednesday simultaneously launched a submarine, a destroyer and a frigate built at a state-run shipyard, underscoring the importance of protecting the Indian Ocean region through which 95% of the country's trade moves amid a strong Chinese presence.
item.dc:creator: RAFIQ MAQBOOL and ASHOK SHARMA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Germany's economy shrank for the second consecutive year in 2024
item.description: The German economy shrank for the second straight year in 2024 as worried consumers held back on spending and Chinese competition ate into the country’s traditional exports of cars and industrial machinery.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Polish leader vows to use EU presidency to speed up Ukraine’s membership quest
item.description: Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk has vowed to use his country’s presidency of the European Union to push forward with Ukraine’s membership quest.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bangladesh Supreme Court acquits ex-Prime Minister Zia, clearing the way for her to run in elections
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Bangladesh’s Supreme Court on Wednesday acquitted former Prime Minister Khaleda Zia in the last corruption case against her, paving the way for her to run in elections that an interim government says will be held either in December or in the first half of 2026.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 5 killed in Mexican town caught between battling criminal groups
item.description: HUITZILAC, Mexico (AP) — Schools and some businesses were closed and few people walked the streets of this town south of Mexico’s capital Tuesday, hours after five people were gunned down on the same street where another attack left eight dead just eight months earlier.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australia's prime minister demands Russia explain what happened to Australian POW
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia’s Prime Minister Anthony Albanese said on Wednesday his government will take the “strongest action possible” if Russia has harmed an Australian who was taken prisoner while fighting for Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean president Yoon Suk Yeol arrested: report
item.description: Disgraced South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol was reportedly arrested over insurrection charges stemming from last month's shocking martial law declaration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada seeks minerals partnership with US amid tariff threats
item.description: Where President-elect Trump sees a rivalry, Canada sees a potential friend. America’s neighbor to the north this week is floating an energy and minerals partnership with the U.S., Canadian Energy and Natural Resources Minister Jonathan Wilkinson told The Hill on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, The Hill

item.title: South Korean authorities take impeached President Yoon to detention center after questioning
item.description: Impeached South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol has been taken to a detention center near Seoul over his imposition of martial law last month.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden to lift Cuba terrorism designation, reversing Trump decision in final week in office
item.description: The Biden administration announced on Tuesday that it will remove Cuba from the list of State Sponsors of Terrorism (SST) in exchange for the release of political prisoners jailed for protesting against the regime in July 2021.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Nearly half worldwide hold antisemitic views: ADL
item.description: Nearly half of people worldwide hold antisemitic views, according to a new survey by the Anti-Defamation League (ADL).
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: VIDEO: 84-year-old man fights off robber using pair of jeans
item.description: YORKSHIRE, U.K. (WFLA) — An 84-year-old man used a pair of jeans to fight off a would-be robber as he did his laundry in South Yorkshire.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: France’s new prime minister says he'll renegotiate a contested plan to raise the retirement age
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France’s new prime minister François Bayrou announced Tuesday the renegotiation of a contested plan raising the retirement age from 62 to 64, in a crucial move to seek more stability for his minority government.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN migration agency says gang violence in Haiti leads to tripling of internally displaced people
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — The U.N. migration agency says internal displacement in Haiti, largely caused by gang violence, has tripled over the last year and now surpasses 1 million people — a record in the Caribbean nation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy, Macron discuss Western troop deployment in Ukraine before German defense chief visits
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said that he has held further discussions with French President Emmanuel Macron about the possibility of Western troops deploying in Ukraine to safeguard any peace deal ending the nearly three-year war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: YEHOR KONOVALOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada prepared to buy more from US amid Trump tariff threats: Ambassador
item.description: Canada’s Ambassador to the U.S. affirmed that the country is willing to boost American imports in the wake of President-elect Trump’s threats to increase tariffs. “We are happy to source what we can from the United States.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Irregular migration into the European Union fell sharply last year, border agency says
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — The number of irregular border crossings into the European Union fell significantly in 2024, according to the bloc's border control agency Frontex, something which it attributed to intensified cooperation against smuggling networks.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: After economic meltdown and war with Israel, Lebanon's new prime minister vows to rebuild
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Lebanon’s prime minister-designate vowed Tuesday to work on building a modern state in the crisis-hit country, saying his priorities will be to rebuild the destruction caused by a yearlong war with Israel and work on pulling the small nation out of its historic economic meltdown.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Malaysia is home to the world's largest hotel, with over 7K rooms for guests
item.description: The Guinness World Record for the largest hotel by number of rooms goes to First World Hotel in Malaysia. The hotel boasts over 7,000 hotel rooms for guests to lounge in.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China reportedly building 'D-Day'-style barges as fears of Taiwan invasion rise
item.description: China is reportedly building a series of barges for amphibious landings amid fears of an invasion of Taiwan, its island neighbor, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Significant progress made in talks over Israel-Hamas ceasefire and hostage release, officials say
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — U.S. and Arab mediators made significant progress overnight toward brokering a ceasefire in the Israel-Hamas war and the release of scores of hostages held in the Gaza Strip, but a deal hasn't been reached yet, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: VICTORIA EASTWOOD, SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Jake Sullivan: 'We are so close' to ceasefire, hostage deal in Gaza
item.description: White House national security adviser Jake Sullivan said Monday the U.S. is “so close to a ceasefire and hostage deal” in Gaza, as President Biden races to secure an agreement on a major foreign policy objective before leaving office in a week.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: China's exports in December up 10.7%, beating estimates as higher US tariffs loom
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — China’s exports in December grew at a faster pace than expected, as factories rushed to fill orders to beat higher tariffs that U.S. President-elect Donald Trump has threatened to impose once he takes office.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO and ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's talk of expansion puts world leaders on alert
item.description: President-elect Trump’s talk of territorial expansion has rattled world leaders at an already precarious time in global politics.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine has captured 2 North Korean soldiers, South Korea's intelligence service says
item.description: Ukraine captured two wounded North Korean soldiers who were fighting on behalf of Russia in a Russian border region, South Korea’s intelligence service said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
